diagnosis.summary: 113/116 (97.4%) of routed balls physically exit the hub and reach the neutral zone (median 0.96 s after release). 3/116 (2.6%, all blue side: lane 3 x2, lane 1 x1) wedge on hub geometry at z~0.97-1.01 and are permanently lost because blocked_until_clear only clears at z<0.40 or |y|<2.70.
root_causes: The ballistic release path grazes the chute ceiling with only +1.3 mm clearance at t=0.04 s (y=-/+3.431, z=1.009) on every lane; lane-dependent lower-cone lips leave +2.7 mm (blue lane 3) to +27 mm. Contact during exit is therefore normal, not exceptional.

HubRouter.step sets only the LINEAR velocity at release; residual ANGULAR velocity from the overlapping holding-pen collisions is preserved. With FuelFoam friction (static 10 / dynamic 5), a spinning ball grazing the +1.3 mm ceiling receives a large random friction impulse: observed speeds 0.02 s after release were 1.35-2.85 m/s versus the commanded 1.003 m/s. Most balls still power through; occasionally one deflects into a chute pocket and wedges (rest points (0.461,-3.476,1.012), (0.375,-3.359,0.967), (0.013,-3.394,0.981)).

The two hub collision meshes are 180-degree-rotated copies, not y-mirrored: the tight web face sits near red lane 1 / blue lane 2 (ballistic path even intersects it by 21-24 mm mid-flight; balls bounce off and still clear, but slowest clears 5.5-5.7 s were blue lanes 1/2), while blue lane 3 has the tightest lower lip (+2.7 mm) - matching where the wedges occurred.
2 of the 3 stuck balls rest INSIDE the blue sensor volume but stay in blocked_until_clear forever (unblock requires z<0.40 or |y|<2.70), so they can never be re-routed: dead fuel for the match.
recommended_fixes: 1) In HubRouter.step release branch, also zero the angular velocity: view.set_angular_velocities(np.zeros((1,3),np.float32), indices=indices). Removes the random spin-friction kick at the ceiling graze; highest-leverage fix, keeps existing parameters.

2) Add an unstick watchdog: track released indices for ~4 s; if speed<0.05 m/s while z>0.30 and |y|>2.70 persists >1 s, re-queue the ball (back to holding pen with a fresh delay and a different exit lane). Makes the pipeline lossless regardless of mesh pockets.

3) Keep exit z=1.02 and velocity (0,-/+1.0,-0.08): grid search over z0 in [0.98,1.02], vy in [1.0,1.5], vz in [-0.08,-0.30] found NO contact-free corridor (best worst-case in-chute clearance -13.6 mm at z0=1.01, vy=1.2); current values are near-optimal for the ceiling (+21.9 mm at spawn). Do not lower z below 1.00 (increases cone contact).
4) Optional: use a lower-friction physics material for the hub chute region if wedging persists after (1)-(2); fuel-vs-chute mu_effective ~5+ currently amplifies any graze.
